base_framework.name: AgentJet
base_framework.takeaway: Decoupled swarm server/client design trains heterogeneous, non-shared-parameter multi-agent teams, with a context-merging timeline module for 1.5-10x rollout speedup.

base_framework.name: HarnessX
base_framework.takeaway: Interleaves trace-driven harness evolution with model RL over a shared replay buffer, co-improving the agent scaffold and the policy.

base_framework.name: Polar
base_framework.takeaway: Treats any real agent harness (Codex, Claude Code, shell) as the RL environment with token-faithful rollouts, so GRPO improves the exact agent that ships.

base_framework.name: uni-agent
base_framework.takeaway: Unifies the inference and training interaction stack (model-tool-env) so the same execution path serves rollout and RL, with fully-async partial rollout at 1000+ concurrency.

base_framework.name: OpenClaw-RL
base_framework.takeaway: Mines 'next-state' signals (tool outputs, user replies) for label-free async RL, mixing scalar GRPO rewards with token-level on-policy distillation hints (OPD).

base_framework.name: Claw-R1
base_framework.takeaway: Adds a Gateway+DataPool middleware between agents and trainers for universal data collection, multi-dimensional reward eval, and curation of agentic RL data.

base_framework.name: Open-AgentRL
base_framework.takeaway: GRPO-TCR co-trains policy with combined outcome + step-wise reward-model signals while the reward model self-improves via consistency feedback in a closed loop.

base_framework.name: NeMo-RL
base_framework.takeaway: Ray-orchestrated RL library with swappable DTensor (native PyTorch) and Megatron-Core backends scaling from single GPU to multi-node without architecture changes.

base_framework.name: RLinf
base_framework.takeaway: Macro-to-micro flow (M2Flow) auto-decomposes RL workflows over time/space then recomposes optimized execution via context switching and elastic pipelining.

base_framework.name: agent-lightning
base_framework.takeaway: Fully decouples agent execution from RL training and uses a credit-assignment module to turn arbitrary agent trajectories into trainable MDP transitions.

base_framework.name: siiRL
base_framework.takeaway: Fully distributed multi-controller architecture (DistFlow) removes the central-controller bottleneck for near-linear RL scaling up to 1024 GPUs.

base_framework.name: slime
base_framework.takeaway: Treats multi-turn/tool/sandbox agentic workflows as pluggable data-generation steps feeding one Megatron-train + SGLang-rollout loop, no separate agent framework.

base_framework.name: ROLL
base_framework.takeaway: Single-controller RL framework with a rollout scheduler for fine-grained sample lifecycle control and AutoDeviceMapping for flexible resource allocation.

base_framework.name: AReaL
base_framework.takeaway: Fully decoupled async RL: rollout workers generate continuously without waiting while trainers update per batch, removing the slowest-sample sync bottleneck.

base_framework.name: MARTI
base_framework.takeaway: Centralized multi-agent interaction with distributed per-agent policy training, enabling collaborative reasoning while scaling RL across heterogeneous models.

base_framework.name: Tunix
base_framework.takeaway: JAX-native post-training library integrating vLLM/MaxText for efficient distributed RL (PPO/GRPO/DAPO) on TPUs.

base_framework.name: RL2
base_framework.takeaway: Concise LLM post-training library with clear abstraction-free implementations yet 3D/5D parallelism, aimed at learning/quick-testing custom RL algorithms.

base_framework.name: verifiers
base_framework.takeaway: Standardizes RL environments as composable modules bundling dataset + rubric reward + tool/sandbox harness, decoupling task logic from the training framework.

base_framework.name: prime-rl
base_framework.takeaway: Fully asynchronous off-policy RL that decouples inference from training for high-throughput agentic training scaling across 1000+ GPUs.

base_framework.name: oat
base_framework.takeaway: Frames LLM alignment as contextual dueling bandits and uses Thompson-sampling active exploration (SEA) for sample-efficient online preference learning.

base_framework.name: veRL
base_framework.takeaway: HybridFlow blends single- and multi-controller paradigms with a 3D-HybridEngine that reshards the actor between train/generate with zero memory redundancy.

base_framework.name: OpenRLHF
base_framework.takeaway: Ray + vLLM + DeepSpeed integration delivering a simplified, accessible RLHF framework with 1.22x-1.68x speedups over prior systems.

base_framework.name: trl
base_framework.takeaway: Transformers-ecosystem trainer classes (SFT/GRPO/DPO) that abstract post-training algorithms into easy, auto-distributed APIs.

general_multitask.name: T2PO
general_multitask.takeaway: Two-level uncertainty control: triggers thinking interventions when token-level uncertainty gains plateau and resamples turns with negligible progress to cut wasted rollouts.

general_multitask.name: StraTA
general_multitask.takeaway: Samples a compact high-level strategy from the initial state, conditions all actions on it, and jointly trains both via hierarchical GRPO rollouts for consistent long-horizon plans.

general_multitask.name: SDAR
general_multitask.takeaway: On-policy self-distillation as a sigmoid-gated auxiliary objective, amplifying teacher endorsements while softening rejections to stabilize multi-turn agent RL.

general_multitask.name: SkillZero
general_multitask.rl_algorithm: In-Context Agentic RL (GRPO + skill-context curriculum withdrawal)
general_multitask.takeaway: Dynamic curriculum that progressively withdraws in-context skill guidance during RL so skills internalize into model weights for zero-shot agent behavior.

general_multitask.name: MetaClaw
general_multitask.takeaway: Continual meta-learning co-evolving LLM policy and skill library: LLM-evolver synthesizes skills from failures plus gradient updates in idle windows.

general_multitask.name: SkillRL
general_multitask.takeaway: Distills agent experience into a hierarchical, recursively-evolving SkillBank with adaptive retrieval that co-improves the RL policy while cutting token usage.

general_multitask.name: LLM-in-Sandbox
general_multitask.tool_usage: Yes (Code Sandbox w/ Terminal, File, Internet)
general_multitask.takeaway: RL trains LLMs to explore inside a code sandbox using only non-agentic data, eliciting general agentic ability across math/science/long-context/IF domains.

general_multitask.name: youtu-agent
general_multitask.takeaway: Meta-agent auto-generates agent tools/prompts/configs, plus hybrid in-context practice and training-free GRPO that improves agents without weight updates.

general_multitask.name: DEPO
general_multitask.takeaway: Dual-efficiency preference optimization jointly minimizing tokens-per-step and number-of-steps, rewarding concise reasoning and fewer actions in agent tasks.

general_multitask.name: SPEAR
general_multitask.takeaway: Self-imitation with curriculum-scheduled entropy: intrinsic-reward exploration early, exploiting successful trajectories later, avoiding entropy collapse.

general_multitask.name: DeepAgent
general_multitask.takeaway: End-to-end tool-reasoning agent with autonomous memory folding, trained via ToolPO using simulated APIs and tool-call advantage attribution for credit.

general_multitask.name: AgentRL
general_multitask.takeaway: Fully-asynchronous generation-training pipeline plus cross-policy sampling and task advantage normalization for stable multi-turn, multi-task agent RL.

general_multitask.name: AgentGym-RL
general_multitask.takeaway: Trains agents via pure RL without SFT using ScalingInter-RL, which starts with restricted interactions then grows the horizon to prevent behavior collapse.

general_multitask.name: Agent_Foundation_Models
general_multitask.takeaway: Consolidates multi-agent systems into one model via multi-agent distillation into SFT trajectories plus agentic RL on chain-of-agents reasoning.

general_multitask.name: Trinity-RFT
general_multitask.takeaway: Unified modular RFT framework spanning sync/async, on/off-policy, and online/offline modes with integrated agent-environment data pipelines.

general_multitask.name: SPA-RL-Agent
general_multitask.takeaway: Stepwise Progress Attribution: a learned progress estimator decomposes delayed task reward into per-step intermediate rewards for credit assignment in agent RL.

general_multitask.name: verl-agent
general_multitask.takeaway: GiGPO: two-level advantage (episode groups + anchor-state step groups) for fine-grained multi-turn credit assignment without extra models or rollouts.

general_multitask.name: SkyRL
general_multitask.takeaway: Optimized async pipeline dispatcher (1.55x speedup) plus AST-based tool-enhanced training, cutting long-horizon agent RL cost over 2x.

general_multitask.name: VAGEN
general_multitask.takeaway: VLM agents trained as POMDP with explicit state-estimation/transition reasoning, a World Modeling Reward, and Bi-Level GAE for turn-aware credit assignment.

general_multitask.name: ART
general_multitask.takeaway: Client-server GRPO trainer adding RL to existing agents without GPU-infra hassle, plus RULER for automatic LLM-judged reward generation.

general_multitask.name: OpenManus-RL
general_multitask.takeaway: Open RL framework for LLM agents combining reasoning paradigms, diverse rollouts, agent reward models, and test-time trajectory scaling (PPO/DPO/GRPO).

search_rag.name: Harness-1
search_rag.takeaway: Externalizes search state (candidate pool, evidence, verification records) into a stateful harness so a small 20B policy can run long-horizon retrieval.

search_rag.name: SlimSearcher
search_rag.takeaway: Adaptive reward gating cascades a correctness gate with tool/token-efficiency rewards, cutting deep-research tool-call rounds 17-58% without losing accuracy.

search_rag.name: DeepRubric
search_rag.takeaway: Recursive sub-question expansion turns deep-research supervision into an evidence tree of atomic verifiable rubric leaves, cutting RL GPU-hours ~13x.

search_rag.name: SAAS
search_rag.task: Self-aware agentic search (over-search mitigation, 7 QA sets)
search_rag.takeaway: Boundary-aware reward teaches the agent when NOT to search, mitigating over-search while keeping minimal sufficient retrieval.

search_rag.name: CuSearch
search_rag.takeaway: Search-depth-aware curriculum rollout reallocates RL update budget toward deeper-search trajectories, lifting multi-hop EM from outcome-only supervision.

search_rag.name: ORBIT
search_rag.task: Verifiable data-gen + RL for web search (Qwen3-4B)
search_rag.takeaway: Generates 20K verifiable reasoning-intensive web-QA queries at near-zero API cost to train search agents with GRPO on a tight budget.

search_rag.name: LiteResearcher
search_rag.rl_algorithm: Scalable Agentic RL (curriculum w/ lite virtual world)
search_rag.takeaway: Trains deep-research agents in a 'lite virtual world' mirroring real search dynamics, avoiding the instability and cost of live-search RL.

search_rag.name: DR-Venus
search_rag.rl_algorithm: GRPO + IGPO (info-gain turn-level) w/ agentic SFT
search_rag.takeaway: Edge-scale (4B) deep-research agent trained with info-gain turn-level rewards plus format-aware regularization to densify supervision and credit assignment.

search_rag.name: MR-Search
search_rag.takeaway: In-context meta-RL conditions each search episode on past episodes plus a self-generated reflection, giving fine-grained multi-episode credit.

search_rag.name: ProRAG
search_rag.takeaway: GRPO with dual-granularity advantage combining PRM step-level process rewards and global outcome signals to curb process hallucinations in multi-hop RAG.

search_rag.name: O-Researcher
search_rag.takeaway: Two-stage SFT-then-RL pipeline over multi-agent synthesized data to boost open-source deep-research agents, combining GRPO with RLAIF for alignment.

search_rag.name: Agentic-RAG-R1
search_rag.takeaway: Applies GRPO with composite accuracy/format/RAGAS rewards to jointly optimize when to retrieve and which reasoning steps to take in knowledge-intensive QA.

search_rag.name: MemSearcher
search_rag.takeaway: Maintains a compact question-relevant memory across turns (stable context length), trained with multi-context GRPO propagating advantages across all turns.

search_rag.name: DR Tulu
search_rag.takeaway: Reinforcement Learning with Evolving Rubrics (RLER): evaluation rubrics co-evolve with the policy to give discriminative on-policy feedback for long-form research without verifiable answers.

search_rag.name: IGPO
search_rag.takeaway: Derives dense intrinsic turn-level rewards from the policy's own belief update (gain in P(correct answer)), curing advantage collapse in multi-turn search RL.

search_rag.name: ReSeek
search_rag.takeaway: Adds a JUDGE self-correction action plus decomposed correctness+utility dense rewards so search agents can re-plan mid-episode instead of locking into bad paths.

search_rag.name: AutoGraph-R1
search_rag.takeaway: Trains an LLM KG constructor with RL where reward is the graph's downstream RAG utility, optimizing for usefulness rather than intrinsic graph quality.

search_rag.name: DeepResearch
search_rag.takeaway: Tongyi DeepResearch couples agentic mid-training and post-training with a fully automatic data-synthesis pipeline, removing human annotation for long-horizon research RL.

search_rag.name: WebSeer
search_rag.takeaway: Self-reflection-enhanced RL with a unified cold-start + RL two-stage framework that lengthens tool-use trajectories while reducing accumulated errors.

search_rag.name: HiPRAG
search_rag.takeaway: Hierarchical process rewards judge the necessity of each search step on-the-fly, adding a bonus for optimal search/non-search ratio to curb over- and under-search.

search_rag.name: Tree-GRPO
search_rag.takeaway: Tree-structured rollouts share prefixes to fit more trajectories per budget and yield step-wise process signals from outcome-only rewards via dual-level advantages.

search_rag.name: DeepDive
search_rag.takeaway: End-to-end multi-turn RL for web-browsing agents with a redundancy penalty that discourages repeated similar queries, enabling longer reasoning chains.

search_rag.name: ASearcher
search_rag.org: Ant Research RL Lab  /  Tsinghua University & UW
search_rag.takeaway: Fully asynchronous RL plus autonomous QA synthesis enables 100+ turn, 400k-token search training, showing horizon length beats architecture complexity.

search_rag.name: SSRL
search_rag.takeaway: Self-Search RL elicits the LLM's own internal knowledge as a simulated search engine via format/rule rewards, cutting external API cost with sim-to-real transfer.

search_rag.name: Research-Venus
search_rag.takeaway: Atomic Thought Rewards decompose agent reasoning into functional units scored by reasoning reward models, giving process-level signal early before switching to outcome rewards.

search_rag.name: Graph-R1
search_rag.takeaway: Treats hypergraph RAG retrieval as a multi-turn agent-environment loop optimized end-to-end by RL, replacing fixed one-shot GraphRAG lookups.

search_rag.name: Kimi-Researcher
search_rag.takeaway: End-to-end REINFORCE research agent with strict on-policy rollouts, negative-sample dropping to avoid entropy collapse, and gamma-decay rewards favoring shorter paths.

search_rag.name: R-Search
search_rag.takeaway: Learns optimal reasoning-search trajectories via multi-reward RL (GRPO/PPO) that jointly balances reasoning quality and search effectiveness.

search_rag.name: R1-Searcher-plus
search_rag.takeaway: SFT cold-start then RL that rewards using internal knowledge and memorizes retrieved facts, so the agent learns when to search vs. recall and needs less retrieval over time.

search_rag.name: StepSearch
search_rag.takeaway: Step-wise PPO with sub-question trajectories and token-level information-gain plus redundancy-penalty rewards to fix sparse rewards in multi-hop search QA.

search_rag.name: AutoRefine
search_rag.takeaway: Search-and-refine-during-think: inserts explicit knowledge-refinement steps between searches, trained with GRPO using both retrieval and answer rewards.

search_rag.name: ZeroSearch
search_rag.takeaway: Replaces costly live search APIs during RL with an SFT'd LLM that generates documents whose quality is curriculum-degraded to progressively elicit reasoning.

search_rag.name: ReasonRAG
search_rag.takeaway: Uses an auto-built process-level reward dataset (query/evidence/answer stages) for process-supervised RAG policy optimization, beating Search-R1 with ~5k vs 90k instances.

search_rag.name: VRAG
search_rag.takeaway: RL for VLMs over visually-rich docs with a crop/scale action space and a reward fusing query rewriting and retrieval performance for coarse-to-fine evidence gathering.

search_rag.name: MaskSearch
search_rag.takeaway: RAMP pre-training: agents learn to call search to fill masked spans at scale, acquiring universal retrieval-and-reasoning skill before DAPO fine-tuning.

search_rag.name: R3-RAG
search_rag.takeaway: Multi-hop RAG RL combining outcome reward (answer correctness) with a process reward from relevance-based document verification to learn iterative reason-retrieve loops.

search_rag.name: O2-Searcher
search_rag.takeaway: Unified reward design trains a 3B agent to distinguish open- vs closed-ended questions and adapt its search/answer strategy, trained in a local simulated search environment.

search_rag.name: s3
search_rag.takeaway: Trains a decoupled search-only agent with a Gain-Beyond-RAG reward (accuracy improvement over naive RAG), leaving the generator LLM frozen.

search_rag.name: knowledge-r1
search_rag.takeaway: Knowledge-boundary-aware reward (IKEA) rewards using internal parametric knowledge first and searching only when insufficient, reducing over-retrieval.

search_rag.name: WebThinker
search_rag.takeaway: Deep Web Explorer with autonomous think-search-draft, trained via iterative online DPO so a reasoning model fills knowledge gaps mid-reasoning.

search_rag.name: DeepResearcher
search_rag.takeaway: End-to-end RL trained directly in the noisy live open web (not a fixed corpus), with browsing agents that cross-validate and self-reflect for robust research.

search_rag.name: Search-R1
search_rag.takeaway: RL teaches LLMs to interleave multi-turn search queries with reasoning, using retrieved-token masking to keep training stable.

search_rag.name: R1-Searcher
search_rag.takeaway: Two-stage outcome-only RL that incentivizes external search without any process rewards or distillation cold-start.

search_rag.name: DeepRetrieval
search_rag.takeaway: RL trains query-generation LLMs using retrieval metrics (e.g. recall) directly as reward, no supervised query data, with a 3B model beating GPT-4o on most IR tasks.

search_rag.name: C-3PO
search_rag.takeaway: Lightweight proxy-centric multi-agent layer over a frozen retriever+LLM, trained via tree-structured rollouts for RL credit assignment across the agents.

web_gui.name: SCALE-CUA
web_gui.task: Computer Use (OSWorld, ScienceBoard; 24K+ synthesized verifiable tasks)
web_gui.rl_algorithm: GRPO (fully async, Frontier Sampling + visual context segmentation)
web_gui.takeaway: Scales computer-use RL by synthesizing 24K verifiable tasks with a proposer/judger/checker loop and cutting rollout cost 2.8x via visual context segmentation.

web_gui.name: OpenWebRL
web_gui.task: Visual web browsing on live sites (WebVoyager/Online-Mind2Web)
web_gui.takeaway: Demystifies online multi-turn RL for visual web agents on live websites with a format + LLM-judge reward over a Playwright sandbox.

web_gui.name: ToolCUA
web_gui.rl_algorithm: Tool-Bootstrapped GUI RFT + Online Agentic RL (Tool-Efficient Path Reward)
web_gui.takeaway: Staged GUI+tool training: synthesized interleaved trajectories, tool-bootstrapped RFT warmup, then online RL with a Tool-Efficient Path Reward for when to switch GUI vs tool calls.

web_gui.name: ClawGUI
web_gui.takeaway: Combines GiGPO with a Process Reward Model for dense step-level supervision to stabilize mobile-GUI agent RL against environment instability.

web_gui.name: OpAgent
web_gui.takeaway: Online agentic RL over a high-concurrency Playwright engine with a hybrid RDTree process reward plus a WebJudge VLM outcome score (WebArena SOTA).

web_gui.name: GUI-Libra
web_gui.takeaway: Action-aware SFT plus partially-verifiable KL-trust-region GRPO to handle GUI states where multiple actions are correct but only one is demonstrated.

web_gui.name: MobileAgent
web_gui.takeaway: Semi-online RL simulates online training on offline trajectories via a Patch Module that corrects rollout-vs-expert divergence, plus discounted future returns for episode-level signal.

web_gui.name: MobileRL
web_gui.takeaway: AdaGRPO with difficulty-adaptive positive replay, failure curriculum filtering, and shortest-path reward reshaping to stabilize multi-turn mobile GUI RL.

web_gui.name: DART-GUI
web_gui.takeaway: Decoupled async RL (env/rollout/data-manager/trainer modules) plus adaptive data curation training on high-entropy decision steps for OSWorld agents.

web_gui.name: Mano-P
web_gui.takeaway: Three-stage SFT to offline RL to online RL pipeline with a simulated data-generation environment and a verification-based error-recovery module.

web_gui.name: InfiGUI-G1
web_gui.takeaway: Adaptive Exploration Policy Optimization (AEPO) uses multi-answer generation plus a theoretically grounded Adaptive Exploration Reward to improve semantic GUI grounding.

web_gui.name: gui-rcpo
web_gui.takeaway: Label-free test-time RL: spatial voting grids over multiple sampled predictions yield consensus-region rewards (RCPO) to self-improve GUI grounding without ground truth.

web_gui.name: MagicGUI
web_gui.takeaway: Iterative two-stage training: large-scale continue-pretrain then RFT with a spatially-enhanced composite reward and dual filtering of RL signals.

web_gui.name: UI-AGILE
web_gui.takeaway: Continuous grounding reward plus a 'Simple Thinking' reward balancing planning quality vs speed, with cropping-based resampling to fix sparse rewards in GUI grounding.

web_gui.name: GUI-G2
web_gui.takeaway: Replaces sparse binary grounding rewards with continuous Gaussian point + coverage rewards giving dense gradients toward optimal click positions.

web_gui.name: GTA1
web_gui.takeaway: Pairs test-time scaling (sample multiple action proposals, judge-model selection) with click-success RL for GUI grounding without explicit thinking.

web_gui.name: Grounding-R1
web_gui.takeaway: GRPO rewards any click landing inside the target element's bounding box rather than the exact center as SFT does, aligning the objective with real user interaction.

web_gui.name: AgentCPM-GUI
web_gui.takeaway: Applies GRPO reinforcement fine-tuning after imitation learning to improve reasoning and generalization to unseen mobile-GUI layouts.

web_gui.name: TTI
web_gui.takeaway: Test-Time Interaction: curriculum-based online RL that adaptively grows rollout length, scaling 'doing' (exploration/backtracking) instead of just 'thinking' for web agents.

web_gui.name: SE-GUI
web_gui.takeaway: Self-evolutionary RFT for GUI grounding: dense continuous-accuracy policy gradient plus attention-map-guided iterative refinement from a small seed set.

web_gui.name: ARPO
web_gui.takeaway: Augments GRPO with a replay buffer of successful trajectories plus baseline-performance task filtering to stabilize long-horizon GUI-agent RL.

web_gui.name: GUI-G1
web_gui.takeaway: Fixes R1-Zero GUI RL failure modes via fast-thinking template, box-size reward constraint to block reward hacking, and difficulty-aware objective scaling.

web_gui.name: WebAgent-R1
web_gui.takeaway: End-to-end multi-turn web RL using only binary task-success rewards with asynchronous diverse trajectory generation, avoiding reward shaping.

web_gui.name: ZeroGUI
web_gui.takeaway: Zero-human-cost online GUI RL using VLMs for automatic task generation and reward estimation, plus two-stage online learning.

web_gui.name: GUI-R1
web_gui.takeaway: Unified action-space rule-based GRPO for GUI agents, matching prior SOTA with only ~3K curated examples vs 13M.

web_gui.name: InfiGUI-R1
web_gui.takeaway: Deliberation enhancement via sub-goal-guidance rewards plus constructed error-recovery scenarios, moving GUI agents from reactive to planning behavior.

web_gui.name: UI-R1
web_gui.takeaway: First to adapt DeepSeek-R1-style rule-based RL to multimodal GUI agents via a rule-based action reward optimized with GRPO.

web_gui.name: CollabUIAgents
web_gui.takeaway: Multi-agent credit re-assignment using LLM-assigned process rewards (not env rewards) so role-free agents learn collaboration that generalizes across environments.

web_gui.name: DigiQ
web_gui.takeaway: Offline TD-learned VLM Q-function on frozen intermediate features, then Best-of-N policy extraction, enabling Android-agent improvement without env interaction.

web_gui.name: GUI-Agent-RL
web_gui.takeaway: VEM: a pretrained Value Environment Model estimates state-action values from offline data to guide policy without env interaction or next-state prediction.

web_gui.name: WebAgent
web_gui.takeaway: Strictly on-policy GRPO with token-level gradients, leave-one-out advantage, and negative-sample filtering to stabilize RL in non-stationary web info-seeking.

web_gui.name: UI-TARS
web_gui.takeaway: Native cross-platform GUI agent bootstrapped by iteratively collecting, filtering, and reflectively refining online traces on hundreds of VMs, with System-2 reasoning.

tool_use.name: Tool-RL-Box
tool_use.task: Multi-step function calling (FCL / ToolACE, pluggable tool servers)
tool_use.takeaway: Diagnoses why multi-step tool-use RL collapses into degenerate formats and fixes it with LLM-judge error classification that regenerates supervisory data mid-training.

tool_use.name: SPADER
tool_use.takeaway: Step-wise Peer Advantage gives critic-free step-level credit, plus a diversity-aware exploration reward for comprehensive multi-answer QA.

tool_use.name: APPO
tool_use.takeaway: Procedure-aware branching extends ARPO with fine-grained credit at tool-call decision points across reasoning, search, and code.

tool_use.name: AgenticQwen
tool_use.task: Industrial Tool Use (search, data analysis, tau-bench airline/retail/telecom)
tool_use.rl_algorithm: Multi-round RL (Reasoning RL + Agentic RL w/ dual data flywheels)
tool_use.tool_usage: Yes (Python interpreter, web search, mock tools)
tool_use.takeaway: Dual data flywheels (reasoning learns from errors; agentic expands linear workflows into branching behavior trees) auto-generate harder tasks for tool-use RL.

tool_use.name: Agent-STAR
tool_use.task: Long-horizon tool-using agents (TravelPlanner, ReAct up to 60 turns)
tool_use.takeaway: A full data-synthesis to SFT to RL recipe with dense curriculum rewards for long-horizon (up to 60-turn) ReAct tool agents on TravelPlanner.

tool_use.name: ToolMaster
tool_use.takeaway: Combines imitation of teacher trajectories with a trial-then-execute phase, then RL, to generalize tool use to novel and unseen tools.

tool_use.name: Agent-R1
tool_use.takeaway: Formalizes LLM-agent components as an extended MDP and provides a modular, extensible end-to-end RL training framework across diverse task scenarios.

tool_use.name: ToolOrchestra
tool_use.takeaway: RL with outcome-, efficiency-, and preference-aware rewards trains a small 8B orchestrator to coordinate tools and larger models more cost-effectively.

tool_use.name: MATPO
tool_use.takeaway: Trains planner and worker roles within a single LLM via role-specific prompts and principled cross-role credit assignment, avoiding separate models.

tool_use.name: AWorld-RL
tool_use.task: Multi-turn function calling + GUI grounding + deep search (BFCL etc.)
tool_use.rl_algorithm: Collection: RODS / HardGen / FunReason-MT / Environment Tuning / V2P / RAG-R1
tool_use.takeaway: Umbrella repo shipping the AWorld team’s agentic-RL recipes (RODS reward-driven online data synthesis, FunReason-MT, Environment Tuning, RAG-R1) with code, data and checkpoints.

tool_use.name: ToolBrain
tool_use.takeaway: Unified tool-use RL framework with LLM-as-a-judge automated reward generation plus GRPO/DPO, distillation, and efficient fine-tuning to avoid manual reward engineering.

tool_use.name: CodeGym
tool_use.takeaway: Converts static coding problems into interactive multi-turn tool-use environments so agents learn diverse tool workflows via RL rather than static-trajectory SFT.

tool_use.name: UserRL
tool_use.takeaway: Standardized gym with simulated users shows reward-shaping and user-simulator choice matter as much as model scale for training interactive user-facing agents.

tool_use.name: Tool-R1
tool_use.takeaway: Maintains a dynamic sample queue caching and reusing high-quality trajectories to cut online sampling cost while RL-training agents to emit executable Python tool code.

tool_use.name: MiroRL
tool_use.takeaway: First RL framework supporting multi-turn MCP tool calls for deep-research agents, integrating search, web, file, and code execution directly into training.

tool_use.name: AWorld
tool_use.takeaway: Tackles experience-generation bottleneck by distributing agent rollouts across a cluster for 14.6x faster experience collection, enabling large-scale agent RL.

tool_use.name: verl-tool
tool_use.takeaway: Tool-as-environment paradigm fully decoupling actor rollout from environment via a unified tool API for native multi-turn tool-calling RL.

tool_use.name: Multi-Turn-RL-Agent
tool_use.takeaway: First systematic study of turn-level reward design for multi-turn agent RL, showing intermediate rewards give finer credit assignment, faster convergence, higher accuracy.

tool_use.name: Tool-N1
tool_use.takeaway: Trains tool-use with a binary rule-based reward judging only format validity and call correctness, letting reasoning emerge without annotated trajectories.

tool_use.name: Tool-Star
tool_use.takeaway: Pairs tool-integrated data synthesis with multi-tool self-critic RL and hierarchical reward design (cold-start SFT then staged RL) for multi-tool coordination.

tool_use.name: RL-Factory
tool_use.takeaway: Decouples environment from RL post-training with async tool-calling, needing only a tool config and reward function while doubling training throughput.

tool_use.name: calculator_agent_rl
tool_use.takeaway: Hybrid reward pairing LLM-as-judge process scoring with code-based answer verification yields large calculator-tool accuracy gains on a 3B model via GRPO.

tool_use.name: ReTool
tool_use.takeaway: Uses outcome-reward RL to interleave real-time code execution within reasoning, letting the model learn strategically when and how to invoke the code tool.

tool_use.name: ToolRL
tool_use.takeaway: Shows coarse answer-matching rewards fail for tool use; principled fine-grained reward design over tools and parameters is essential for effective GRPO training.

tool_use.name: ReCall
tool_use.takeaway: Treats search/retrieval as integral reasoning steps trained via RL (ReSearch), learning when and how to retrieve without supervised reasoning annotations.

code_swe.name: FastContext
code_swe.takeaway: Trains a small parallel-tool-calling repository-explorer subagent so a coding agent gets focused file/line context cheaply via task-grounded RL.

code_swe.name: SWE-Edit
code_swe.takeaway: Decomposes code editing into Viewer and Editor subagents and trains the editor to adaptively pick edit mode rather than always using find-and-replace.

code_swe.name: CodeScout
code_swe.takeaway: RL recipe for code-search agents using a multi-level (file/module/function) F1 reward over a plain Unix terminal toolset.

code_swe.name: CUDA-Agent
code_swe.takeaway: Large-scale agentic RL for CUDA kernels combining data synthesis, a skill-augmented dev environment with automated verification/profiling rewards, and stable-training techniques.

code_swe.name: SWE-World
code_swe.rl_algorithm: RL with learned world model (SWT + SWR)
code_swe.takeaway: Docker-free SWE RL: a learned world model predicts execution outcomes and test feedback, enabling cheap rollouts and test-time trajectory selection.

code_swe.name: CUDA-L2
code_swe.takeaway: Contrastive RL with CUDA execution speed as reward optimizes HGEMM matmul kernels, beating cuBLAS by 19.2% via large-scale config exploration.

code_swe.name: PPP-Agent
code_swe.takeaway: Multi-objective RL (PPP) jointly optimizing productivity, proactivity, and personalization, evaluated in the UserVille simulator with configurable LLM user simulators.

code_swe.name: DeepAnalyze
code_swe.takeaway: Curriculum-based agentic RL plus data-grounded trajectory synthesis trains an 8B model for end-to-end autonomous data science without fixed workflows.

code_swe.name: RepoDeepSearch
code_swe.takeaway: ToolTrain: rejection-sampled SFT plus tool-integrated RL teaches LLMs to navigate repository retrieval tools across multi-step reasoning for bug localization.

code_swe.name: CUDA-L1
code_swe.takeaway: Contrastive RL using execution speedup as the only reward turns a weak LLM into a CUDA optimizer that combines and rejects optimizations.

code_swe.name: digitalhuman
code_swe.takeaway: RLVER: PPO with deterministic emotion scores from self-consistent simulated users as verifiable rewards to train empathetic dialogue agents.

code_swe.name: SWE-Swiss
code_swe.takeaway: Decomposes issue resolution into localization/repair/unit-test skills, trained via multi-task SFT then two-stage RL on repair, plus similarity-based selection.

code_swe.name: MedAgentGym
code_swe.takeaway: Scalable sandboxed environment of 72K biomedical coding tasks with verifiable feedback for multi-turn trajectory sampling and offline/online RL.

code_swe.name: CURE
code_swe.takeaway: Co-evolves a code generator and a unit-test generator via RL with no ground-truth code; the tester learns from the coder's mistakes.

code_swe.name: Time-R1
code_swe.takeaway: Three-stage RL curriculum with a dynamic rule-based reward builds temporal understanding then future prediction, letting a 3B model beat far larger ones.

code_swe.name: ML-Agent
code_swe.takeaway: Online RL for ML-engineering agents using exploration-enriched SFT, step-wise (per-action) RL, and a unified reward unifying heterogeneous ML signals.

code_swe.name: R1-Code-Interpreter
code_swe.takeaway: Multi-stage RL curriculum partitioning samples by improvement potential teaches LLMs to autonomously emit code queries during reasoning.

code_swe.name: Skywork-OR1
code_swe.takeaway: Scalable long-CoT RL on R1-Distill models whose key lesson is mitigating premature entropy collapse to sustain exploration and test accuracy.

code_swe.name: sweet_rl
code_swe.takeaway: SWEET-RL solves multi-turn credit assignment by training a critic with privileged training-time info to give step-level rewards for the policy.

code_swe.name: swe-rl
code_swe.takeaway: RL on open-source software-evolution data with a lightweight rule-based similarity reward to ground-truth PRs; skills generalize beyond SWE.

code_swe.name: CTRL
code_swe.takeaway: Trains a critic via RL to generate feedback maximizing a fixed generator's code-correction performance, with no human-labeled critiques.

code_swe.name: AceCoder
code_swe.takeaway: Auto-synthesized (question, test-case) pairs supply verifiable pass-rate rewards for code RL, removing the need for hand-labeled reward data.

code_swe.name: rllm
code_swe.org: Berkeley Sky Computing Lab  /  BAIR / Together AI
code_swe.takeaway: Framework (verl fork) for post-training custom language agents and environments with RL; recipes for DeepSWE, DeepCoder, DeepScaleR.

code_swe.name: open-r1
code_swe.takeaway: Fully open reproduction of DeepSeek-R1: GRPO plus distillation/R1-Zero pipeline with code-execution sandboxes as verifiable reward signals.

reasoning.name: Agent0
reasoning.org: UNC‑Chapel Hill / Salesforce Research / Stanford University
reasoning.takeaway: Symbiotic co-evolution: a curriculum agent proposes harder tool-aware tasks while an executor agent learns to solve them, from zero external data.

reasoning.name: KG-R1
reasoning.takeaway: Single unified LLM agent with four KG operations trained by GRPO, giving schema-agnostic train-once-transfer-anywhere KGQA at low token cost.

reasoning.name: AgentFlow
reasoning.takeaway: Flow-GRPO trains the planner inside a live multi-turn loop by converting it to single-turn updates and broadcasting the trajectory outcome to every turn.

reasoning.name: ARPO
reasoning.takeaway: Entropy-based adaptive rollout that adds step-level sampling after high-uncertainty tool calls, matching baselines with half the tool-use budget.

reasoning.name: terminal-bench-rl
reasoning.takeaway: GRPO terminal-agent training with a hybrid reward: 65% Docker-verified weighted unit tests plus 35% LLM-judge scoring of planning and tool-use process.

reasoning.name: MOTIF
reasoning.takeaway: Multi-round GRPO that generates thinking tokens across several sequential rounds to expand effective reasoning beyond the context-window limit.

reasoning.name: cmriat/l0
reasoning.takeaway: Code-as-action NB-Agent (REPL) trained purely with RLVR via a low-cost sandboxed concurrent worker pool, lifting factuality QA from a base model with no SFT.

reasoning.name: agent-distillation
reasoning.takeaway: Distills full tool-using agent behavior (not just CoT) into small LMs via first-thought prefix prompting and self-consistent action generation.

reasoning.name: EasyR1
reasoning.takeaway: Efficient multi-modal RL framework (HybridEngine + vLLM SPMD) supporting GRPO/DAPO/RLOO/GSPO etc. for text and vision-language models with padding-free training and LoRA.

reasoning.name: AutoCoA
reasoning.takeaway: SFT+RL trains agent models to internalize Chain-of-Action, using step-level action triggers and an internal world model to cut real-environment interaction cost.

reasoning.name: ToRL
reasoning.takeaway: Tool-integrated RL from a base model lets LLMs discover when to invoke code via reward alone, emergently self-regulating ineffective code and switching reasoning modes.

reasoning.name: ReMA
reasoning.takeaway: Decouples reasoning into a high-level meta-thinking agent and a low-level reasoning agent, co-trained via multi-agent RL to elicit metacognitive planning.

reasoning.name: Agentic-Reasoning
reasoning.takeaway: Augments LLM reasoning with a Mind-Map knowledge-graph agent plus web-search and code agents to keep long tool-heavy reasoning chains coherent.

reasoning.name: SimpleTIR
reasoning.takeaway: Stabilizes multi-turn tool-integrated RL by filtering out 'void' trajectories that yield neither a code block nor a final answer, curbing error accumulation and drift.

reasoning.name: openrlhf_async_pipline
reasoning.takeaway: Asynchronous RLHF pipeline decoupling rollout from training (vLLM hybrid engine, dynamic batch size) to remove GPU idling, since generation is ~80% of RLHF time.

reasoning.name: THOR
reasoning.takeaway: Hierarchical GRPO jointly optimizing episode-level solving and step-level code, exploiting that intermediate tool-call success predicts final-answer correctness.

reasoning.name: Tool-Light
reasoning.takeaway: Self-evolved DPO using entropy-guided self-sampling to teach efficient tool use, fixing insufficient/excessive tool calls and overthinking via the model's own pairs.

reasoning.name: AutoTIR
reasoning.takeaway: PPO with a hybrid reward (answer correctness + format + wrong-tool penalty) lets the model autonomously decide whether and which tool to invoke without fixed patterns.

multi_agent.name: Maestro
multi_agent.task: 10 multimodal benchmarks (math/chart/HR/domain — 70.1% avg, beats GPT-5 & Gemini-2.5-Pro)
multi_agent.rl_algorithm: Outcome RL (lightweight orchestrator over frozen expert ensembles)
multi_agent.takeaway: Outcome-only RL trains a lightweight orchestrator over a frozen expert ensemble to decide which model-skill to invoke and when to stop, generalizing to unseen experts.

multi_agent.name: PettingLLMs
multi_agent.takeaway: AT-GRPO adds agent- and turn-wise advantage grouping because standard GRPO grouping breaks when prompts vary by role and turn, enabling stable multi-agent RL.

multi_agent.name: MASPRM
multi_agent.takeaway: Process reward model for multi-agent systems trained from MCTS rollouts with only terminal rewards propagated to local targets, scoring partial transcripts to guide search.

multi_agent.name: ARIA
multi_agent.takeaway: Projects language actions into a low-dimensional intention space so semantically similar actions share rewards, densifying sparse signals and cutting gradient variance.

multi_agent.name: AMPO
multi_agent.takeaway: Adaptive Mode Policy Optimization lets social agents switch reasoning depth (intuitive to deliberate) per context, yielding 33% shorter chains with higher performance.

multi_agent.name: MAPoRL
multi_agent.takeaway: Cooperative multi-agent RL co-training heterogeneous LLMs via PPO with a verifier reward, so different models specialize and learn from each other on collaborative tasks.

multi_agent.name: FlowReasoner
multi_agent.takeaway: Reasoning meta-agent (distilled from R1) that generates a per-query multi-agent workflow, refined by RL on execution feedback over performance/complexity/efficiency rewards.

multi_agent.name: DrMAS
multi_agent.takeaway: Agent-wise advantage normalization using each agent's own reward stats, fixing the gradient-norm instability that global GRPO baselines cause in multi-agent LLM systems.

multi_agent.name: MarsRL
multi_agent.takeaway: Jointly RL-trains Solver/Verifier/Corrector agents using agent-specific rewards to cut reward noise plus pipeline-style training for long math trajectories.

multi_agent.name: MrlX
multi_agent.takeaway: M-GRPO optimizes main- and sub-agents separately via group-relative advantages plus trajectory alignment for fixed-size batches, no end-to-end backprop.

multi_agent.name: CoMAS
multi_agent.org: Shanghai AI Lab / CUHK / Oxford / NUS
multi_agent.takeaway: Agents co-evolve via RL using LLM-as-judge intrinsic rewards mined from peer discussion dynamics, needing no external reward signal.

multi_agent.name: CoMLRL
multi_agent.takeaway: Casts LLM collaboration as cooperative MARL and fine-tunes the system with group-level RL (MAGRPO) instead of hand-engineering per-agent rewards.

multi_agent.name: SPIRAL
multi_agent.takeaway: Self-play on zero-sum games as an auto-curriculum, stabilized by Role-conditioned Advantage Estimation that accounts for each player's role.

multi_agent.name: MARFT
multi_agent.takeaway: Reformulates multi-agent fine-tuning as a Flex-MG game with an algorithm tailored to LLM-based MAS rather than directly porting classical MARL.

memory.name: Supersede
memory.task: Memory-update gap: keeping notes current across sessions (LongMemEval knowledge-update)
memory.tool_usage: Yes (capped notes memory as action space)
memory.takeaway: Isolates the memory-update gap - agents citing superseded facts - as a verifiable RL task, rewarding current answers and penalizing stale ones across multi-session notes.

memory.name: AgeMem
memory.task: Unified LTM/STM management (memory ops as tools)
memory.takeaway: Exposes store/retrieve/update/summarize/discard as tool actions in the agent's policy, trained with step-wise GRPO to handle sparse memory-op rewards.

memory.name: MEM1
memory.takeaway: Maintains a constant-size shared internal state that consolidates memory and reasoning, discarding irrelevant context for efficient long-horizon agents.

memory.name: Memento
memory.takeaway: Memory-augmented MDP that adapts via episodic case retrieval and a learned case-selection policy, with no gradient updates to the underlying LLM.

memory.name: MemAgent
memory.takeaway: RL-trains an overwrite-based segment memory (DAPO extended to multi-conversation generation), extrapolating from 8K to 3.5M tokens with little degradation.

memory.name: Mem-alpha
memory.takeaway: RL teaches agents what to store, how to structure, and when to update memory, optimized via downstream QA reward instead of fixed memory heuristics.

memory.name: M3-Agent
memory.takeaway: Builds entity-centric multimodal episodic and semantic memory from streaming audio-visual input, retrieved during multi-turn reasoning for long-video QA.

embodied.name: REAL
embodied.task: Open-world mobile manipulation in Isaac Sim (REAL-Bench, 241 tasks)
embodied.rl_algorithm: GRPO/GSPO (online RL over an MCP tool interface)
embodied.takeaway: Exposes embodied skills as MCP tools (including asking the user) so a language agent can be RL-trained on open-world mobile manipulation and deployed on real robots.

embodied.name: Embodied-R1.5
embodied.task: Embodied foundation model w/ Planner-Grounder-Corrector closed-loop
embodied.takeaway: A single 8B embodied foundation model plays Planner-Grounder-Corrector in a closed loop, RFT-tuned with a multi-task balanced recipe to self-correct long-horizon real-robot tasks.

embodied.name: AVA-VLA
embodied.task: VLA manipulation (LIBERO/ALOHA), latent CoT w/ early-exit
embodied.takeaway: Casts latent reasoning as a sequential decision process and adds adaptive early-exit, optimizing VLA reasoning trajectories with task and trajectory-consistency rewards.

embodied.name: WorldVLN
embodied.takeaway: Action-aware GRPO on an autoregressive world-action model closes the loop for aerial vision-language navigation by re-encoding observations after each action.

embodied.name: Embodied-R1
embodied.takeaway: Uses embodiment-agnostic 'pointing' as intermediate action representation, trained via two-stage RFT with multi-task rewards for cross-robot transfer.

embodied.name: VIKI-R
embodied.takeaway: SFT on CoT-annotated demos then GRPO under multi-level rewards, grounding multi-robot cooperative reasoning directly in visual input.

embodied.name: STeCa
embodied.takeaway: Detects bad steps via step-level reward comparison during exploration and uses LLM reflection to build corrected trajectories for calibration before RFT.

domain_specific.name: FaithMed
domain_specific.rl_algorithm: SFT (LLaMA-Factory) + agentic RL w/ process reward
domain_specific.takeaway: Rewards not just the right medical answer but whether each reasoning step is grounded in retrieved evidence, using step-level process rewards over search trajectories.

domain_specific.name: Gene-Disease-Curation
domain_specific.takeaway: Process-supervised hierarchical multi-agent RL improves clinical gene-disease validity curation, with process+outcome rewards lifting process F1 0.39->0.52 on ClinGen.

domain_specific.name: MedSAM-Agent
domain_specific.takeaway: Reframes interactive segmentation as multi-turn decision-making, trained on expert trajectories with process rewards favoring parsimonious refinement.

domain_specific.name: ChemCraft
domain_specific.task: Chemical LM orchestrating chemistry tools (molecular design/synthesis)
domain_specific.takeaway: SMILES-GRPO trains a chemical language model to orchestrate chemistry tools in an agent sandbox for molecular design and synthesis-pathway prediction.

domain_specific.name: Alpha-R1
domain_specific.takeaway: RL-trained 8B reasoner judges alpha-factor relevance from factor logic and real-time news, toggling factors via economic rationale to fight signal decay.

domain_specific.name: Doctor-R1
domain_specific.takeaway: Experiential agentic RL with a two-tier reward separating clinical decisions from communication, grounded by a repository of high-quality trajectories.

domain_specific.name: OS-R1
domain_specific.takeaway: Abstracts Linux kernel tuning as an RL environment with rule-based rewards for performance-aware reasoning, plus two-phase training to speed convergence.

domain_specific.name: MedResearcher-R1
domain_specific.takeaway: Mines longest knowledge-graph chains around rare medical entities to synthesize multi-hop QA, then SFT + online RL with a private medical retriever.

domain_specific.name: LegalDelta
domain_specific.takeaway: Uses the information gap between direct-answer and reasoning-augmented modes as a GRPO reward signal, with structural+legal-domain reward, no preference labels.

domain_specific.name: MMedAgent-RL
domain_specific.takeaway: RL-trains triage and attending GP agents for medical VQA with curriculum learning and dynamic entropy so the attending balances trust vs correcting specialists.

domain_specific.name: DoctorAgent-RL
domain_specific.takeaway: Models consultation as multi-turn RL that learns a questioning strategy to actively elicit key patient info, rather than imitating supervised dialogue.

domain_specific.name: Biomni
domain_specific.takeaway: General biomedical agent combining LLM reasoning, retrieval-augmented planning, and code execution; Biomni-R0 adds RL from agent-interaction data on Qwen-32B.

reward_training.name: AgentV-RL
reward_training.takeaway: Turns reward modeling into a multi-turn tool-augmented deliberative process with forward+backward verifier agents; a 4B verifier beats SOTA ORMs by 25%.

reward_training.name: DataMind
reward_training.takeaway: An environment-aware generative process reward model (DataPRM) scores intermediate Python/SQL steps and plugs into RL for data-analysis agents.

reward_training.name: ARLArena
reward_training.takeaway: Decomposes the agentic policy gradient into four design axes and proposes SAMPO to prevent training collapse across web, embodied, math, game, and search.

reward_training.name: Agent-RRM
reward_training.task: Reward model for agents (web nav, multi-hop QA)
reward_training.rl_algorithm: Agentic RL w/ trained reasoning RM
reward_training.takeaway: Trains a reasoning reward model that emits structured trajectory critiques and scores, then uses it to train multi-turn tool-use agents.

reward_training.name: ToolPRMBench
reward_training.takeaway: Benchmark for process reward models in tool use: step-level test cases (history, correct vs wrong action, tool metadata) via offline+online sampling.

reward_training.name: RLVR-World
reward_training.takeaway: Trains world models with RLVR by treating decoded-prediction metrics (accuracy, perceptual quality) as verifiable rewards, fixing MLE-metric misalignment.

reward_training.name: AgentProg
reward_training.takeaway: ProgRM predicts per-step task-completion progress (LCS self-annotation of key steps) as dense reward, beating outcome RMs that over-penalize failed GUI trajectories.

reward_training.name: AgentPRM
reward_training.takeaway: Lightweight actor-critic PRM via Monte Carlo rollouts (plus InversePRM learning process rewards from demos), bolting onto existing RLHF pipelines.

reward_training.name: Agentic-Reward-Modeling
reward_training.takeaway: RewardAgent fuses human-preference RM with verifiable factuality and instruction-following signals for more reliable rewards in best-of-n and DPO.

reward_training.name: AgentRM
reward_training.takeaway: Finetuning a reward model to guide policy at test time (best-of-N, step-level beam search) generalizes better across agent tasks than finetuning the policy.

safety.name: SafeSearch
safety.takeaway: Multi-objective RL adding a query-level shaping term that penalizes unsafe search queries, cutting harmfulness >90% while keeping QA utility for search agents.

safety.name: curiosity_redteam
safety.takeaway: Adds curiosity/novelty bonuses to RL red-teaming so the attacker covers a far more diverse span of prompts instead of collapsing to a few effective test cases.

safety.name: RLbreaker
safety.takeaway: Frames jailbreaking as RL-guided search (customized PPO + bespoke reward) instead of random genetic mutation, yielding transferable black-box attacks.

safety.name: xJailbreak
safety.takeaway: RL jailbreak guided by embedding proximity between benign and malicious prompts, giving a denser reward that preserves intent while maximizing attack success.

safety.name: Auto-RT
safety.takeaway: RL red-teaming that optimizes multi-step attack strategies via early-terminated exploration and progressive reward tracking with intermediate downgrade models.

safety.name: ToolSafe
safety.takeaway: TS-Guard, a multi-task RL guardrail that judges tool-call safety from interaction history at each step, cutting harmful invocations ~65% under prompt injection.

safety.name: TROJail
safety.takeaway: Trajectory-level RL for multi-turn jailbreaks: final-turn harmfulness as outcome reward plus refusal-penalty and semantic-steering process rewards for sparse signal.

safety.name: Jailbreak-R1
safety.takeaway: Three-stage GRPO red-teaming (imitation cold-start, diversity+consistency warm-up, progressive jailbreak reward) to balance attack effectiveness and diversity.

safety.name: GuardReasoner-VL
safety.takeaway: Reasoning-based VLM safety guard: SFT cold-start then online RL with length-aware safety reward and dynamic clipping for deliberative multimodal moderation.

vlm_agent.name: VTS
vlm_agent.task: Grounded long-video QA (search video as an adaptive temporal tree)
vlm_agent.takeaway: Treats long-video QA as iterative search over an adaptive temporal tree, training the agent to zoom/shift and to recover from wrong branches instead of one-shot frame sampling.

vlm_agent.name: VSeek
vlm_agent.task: Long-video QA as multi-turn evidence retrieval (LongVideoBench/LVBench/Video-MME/MLVU)
vlm_agent.takeaway: Turns passive single-pass video QA into a think-search-observe loop, with dense rewards from temporal-logic decomposition of the query into retrievable visual primitives.

vlm_agent.name: HyperEyes
vlm_agent.takeaway: Fuses visual grounding and retrieval into one Unified Grounded Search action and searches multiple entities concurrently, training inference efficiency as an objective.

vlm_agent.name: ODE
vlm_agent.takeaway: On-policy data evolution over a 9-tool visual-native harness (visual search, zoom, code) with an image-bank memory for multimodal deep search.

vlm_agent.name: ParaVT
vlm_agent.agent_arity: Multi (main + parallel sub-agents w/ shared weights)
vlm_agent.takeaway: PARA-GRPO for long-video agents: targeted format reward at fragile structural tokens plus frame-budget randomization to stop tool-prior-induced format collapse.

vlm_agent.name: OpenSearch-VL
vlm_agent.tool_usage: Yes (text/image search, OCR, crop, sharpen, SR, perspective)
vlm_agent.takeaway: Multi-turn fatal-aware GRPO masks post-tool-failure tokens while keeping pre-failure reasoning via one-sided advantage clamping for multimodal deep search.

vlm_agent.name: MTA-Agent
vlm_agent.task: Multimodal Deep Search (21K MTA-Vision-DeepSearch; 32B beats GPT-5 54.63%)
vlm_agent.tool_usage: Yes (web search, web read, Google Lens, image search)
vlm_agent.takeaway: DAPO trained by replaying cached tool interactions (no live API calls) on 21K multi-hop data, deepening multimodal search reasoning from 2.27 to 4.28 steps.

vlm_agent.name: Gen-Searcher
vlm_agent.takeaway: Reinforces multi-hop web + visual-reference search before grounded image synthesis, with dual text and image rewards.

vlm_agent.name: MM-DeepResearch
vlm_agent.takeaway: A simple multimodal agentic-search baseline that learns when/how to call image-to-image, text-to-image, and text-to-text retrieval engines via GRPO.

vlm_agent.name: PyVision-RL
vlm_agent.task: Agentic image+video understanding w/ dynamic Python tooling
vlm_agent.takeaway: Writes Python-as-tool on the fly for agentic image and video understanding, with an accumulative per-turn tool reward to prevent interaction collapse.

vlm_agent.name: Vision-DeepResearch
vlm_agent.takeaway: Incentivizes long-horizon multimodal deep research (dozens of turns, hundreds of searches) in an MLLM via cold-start SFT then GRPO.

vlm_agent.name: ARM-Thinker
vlm_agent.takeaway: A multimodal generative reward model that thinks, acts (zoom/crop, doc retrieval, validators), and verifies in an iterative loop, trained with two-stage GRPO.

vlm_agent.name: CodeDance
vlm_agent.task: Executable visual reasoning (visual search/math/chart via code)
vlm_agent.takeaway: A dynamic tool-integrated MLLM that composes code-as-tools (crop, draw, count, plot) over multiple turns for executable visual reasoning, with a balanced adaptive tool-call reward.

vlm_agent.name: DeepEyesV2
vlm_agent.takeaway: Shows direct RL alone fails to induce robust tool use; needs cold-start then RL to get task-adaptive tool invocation (image ops for perception, code for math).

vlm_agent.name: Mini-o3
vlm_agent.takeaway: Over-turn masking avoids penalizing responses that hit the turn cap during GRPO, letting models trained on ~6 turns scale to tens of turns of visual search at inference.

vlm_agent.name: VisionThink
vlm_agent.takeaway: Model starts at low resolution and emits a special token to request a high-res image only when needed; RL with LLM-as-Judge trains this adaptive token-compression behavior.

vlm_agent.name: multimodal-search-r1
vlm_agent.takeaway: First end-to-end RL (GRPO) teaching LMMs on-demand multi-turn image+text search, with search penalty and search-balanced data cutting calls >30%.

vlm_agent.name: AutoVLA
vlm_agent.takeaway: Unifies reasoning and action in one autoregressive model by tokenizing trajectories into discrete actions, then uses GRPO RFT to drop unnecessary reasoning in easy driving scenes.

vlm_agent.name: VDeepEyes
vlm_agent.takeaway: RL trains a VLM to use its own grounding as an intrinsic tool, deciding when/where to zoom into images for active perception, with no SFT cold-start or external APIs.

vlm_agent.name: CoSo
vlm_agent.takeaway: Counterfactual soft RL uses causal influence of each token on the post-processed action to focus exploration on action-critical tokens and suppress redundant ones.

vlm_agent.name: Pixel-Reasoner
vlm_agent.takeaway: Adds pixel-space operations (zoom-in, select-frame) to VLM reasoning, using a curiosity-driven reward to balance exploring these visual ops against text reasoning.

vlm_agent.name: Visual-ARFT
vlm_agent.takeaway: Agentic RFT teaches LVLMs multimodal tool use, browsing the web and writing code to manipulate input images for adaptive multi-step reasoning.

vlm_agent.name: VTool-R1
vlm_agent.takeaway: Integrates Python visual-editing tools into RFT so VLMs learn when/how to insert visual steps in multimodal CoT, trained with outcome-only rewards (no process supervision).

vlm_agent.name: OpenThinkIMG
vlm_agent.takeaway: V-ToolRL lets LVLMs discover tool-use strategies by directly optimizing task success from tool-interaction feedback, packaged in an open framework with standardized vision-tool interfaces.

vlm_agent.name: Chain-of-Focus
vlm_agent.takeaway: Two-stage SFT-then-RL pipeline trains a VLM to iteratively zoom into key image regions, using only answer accuracy and format as outcome rewards.

vlm_agent.name: GRIT
vlm_agent.takeaway: Grounded reasoning interleaves natural language with explicit bbox coordinates; GRPO-GR trains it from only answer-accuracy and format rewards, no reasoning-chain or bbox labels.

vlm_agent.name: AlphaDrive
vlm_agent.takeaway: Brings GRPO to driving planning with four planning-tailored rewards plus a two-stage SFT-then-RL recipe, yielding emergent multimodal planning behaviors.

vlm_agent.name: VSC-RL
vlm_agent.takeaway: Recasts VLM agent control as variational subgoal-conditioned RL via an SGC-ELBO objective that maximizes subgoal return while staying near a reference goal policy for sparse long-horizon tasks.

vlm_agent.name: RL4VLM
vlm_agent.takeaway: Fine-tunes a whole VLM with PPO by prompting CoT reasoning, parsing the text into executable actions, and using environment task rewards as the RL signal.

self_evolution.name: SEED
self_evolution.rl_algorithm: GRPO + self-evolving on-policy distillation (hindsight-skill SFT -> OPD during RL)
self_evolution.takeaway: Converts an agent’s own completed trajectories into hindsight skills and distills them back on-policy during RL, densifying outcome-only supervision without a teacher model.

self_evolution.name: OPID
self_evolution.rl_algorithm: On-policy skill distillation (hierarchical hindsight skills -> token-level dense supervision)
self_evolution.takeaway: Extracts hierarchical episode- and step-level skills from finished rollouts and turns them into token-level dense supervision for long-horizon agents.

self_evolution.name: UCOB
self_evolution.rl_algorithm: Credit-aware on-policy bidirectional self-distillation (skill-conditioned vs skill-free branches)
self_evolution.takeaway: Compares skill-conditioned against skill-free rollouts to credit which skills actually help, then distills bidirectionally so the agent both uses and evolves its skill set.

self_evolution.name: SIRI
self_evolution.takeaway: Self-internalizing RL discovers, validates (paired skill-augmented vs skill-free rollouts), and distills the agent's own skills without any external skill bank.

self_evolution.name: world-knowledge
self_evolution.tool_usage: Yes (web pipeline for env-specific knowledge construction)
self_evolution.takeaway: Outcome reward measures how much an agent's self-generated world knowledge raises downstream success, enabling reward-free spontaneous adaptation to unseen environments at inference.

self_evolution.name: ARISE
self_evolution.task: Reasoning w/ intrinsic skill library (7 Olympiad benchmarks)
self_evolution.rl_algorithm: Hierarchical RL (options + intra-option) w/ skill evolution
self_evolution.takeaway: Hierarchical RL with a persistent skill library that co-evolves with reasoning, using counterfactual credit to reward skill quality.

self_evolution.name: Tool-R0
self_evolution.takeaway: Self-play from zero data: a Generator proposes frontier-difficulty tool-use tasks while a Solver learns to solve them, forming a self-improving loop.

self_evolution.name: MemSkill
self_evolution.takeaway: Treats memory operations as learnable evolvable skills: a controller learns skill selection while a designer reviews failures and refines the skill set in a closed loop.

self_evolution.name: MemRL
self_evolution.takeaway: Non-parametric memory RL decouples stable reasoning from plastic episodic memory, using two-phase retrieval and environmental feedback to surface high-utility strategies without weight updates.

self_evolution.name: AgentEvolver
self_evolution.takeaway: Self-questioning (task generation), self-navigating (experience-reuse exploration), and self-attributing (per-action differentiated rewards) make agent RL self-sufficient and sample-efficient.

self_evolution.name: EvolveR
self_evolution.takeaway: Closed-loop self-improvement: offline self-distillation builds a repository of reusable strategic principles, then online retrieval of those principles drives RL policy updates.

self_evolution.name: SEAgent
self_evolution.takeaway: Self-evolving computer-use agent: a World State Model scores trajectories and a curriculum generates tasks, with GRPO on successes plus adversarial imitation of failure actions.

self_evolution.name: R-Zero
self_evolution.org: Tencent AI Seattle Lab / WashU / UMD
self_evolution.takeaway: Co-evolutionary GRPO where a Challenger is rewarded for posing tasks at the edge of Solver ability and the Solver for solving them, no human data.

self_evolution.name: Absolute-Zero-Reasoner
self_evolution.takeaway: Single model proposes its own tasks and learns from code-execution-verified rewards via TRR++, a self-play loop needing zero external datasets.

self_evolution.name: RAGEN
self_evolution.takeaway: StarPO does trajectory-level agent RL; StarPO-S adds trajectory filtering, critic, and gradient stabilization to escape the 'Echo Trap' reward-variance/gradient-spike failure mode.

self_evolution.name: WebRL
self_evolution.takeaway: Self-evolving online curriculum RL generates new tasks from failed attempts, paired with an outcome-supervised reward model and adaptive RL to train open-source web agents.

environment.name: SETA
environment.task: Terminal agents (4,500+ verifiable Docker envs, Terminal-Bench format)
environment.takeaway: Auto-synthesizes and evolves 4,500+ verifiable terminal RL environments (task.yaml + Dockerfile + tests) in Terminal-Bench format, the largest open terminal RL set to date.

environment.name: OpenAgent
environment.takeaway: Controlled sandbox that perturbs queries, tool schemas, observations and domains at inference to expose how brittle statically trained tool-use agents really are.

environment.name: MobileGym
environment.takeaway: Browser-hosted, fully controllable Android simulator with deterministic verifiable judging and 256-instance parallel rollouts for online mobile-GUI RL.

environment.name: AEnvironment
environment.task: Agentic RL Env Platform (MCP, AReaL-integrated, TAU2/SWE/Terminal-Bench)
environment.takeaway: 'Everything as Environment' abstraction extending MCP into one Environment API so tools, benchmarks, and multi-agent systems plug into RL training uniformly.

environment.name: Gym-Anything
environment.takeaway: Turns any software into a computer-use agent environment with realistic data, tasks, and automatic verifiers (CUA-World ships 200+ real apps in sandboxed VMs).

environment.name: OpenSandbox
environment.takeaway: Multi-language sandbox platform with standardized lifecycle APIs over Docker/K8s for secure agent code execution in RL training and evaluation.

environment.name: OpenEnv
environment.takeaway: Gymnasium-style step()/reset()/state() standard for containerized agentic execution environments, removing per-env custom integration for RL.

environment.name: open-trajectory-gym
environment.takeaway: SFT->online RL->GEPA post-training pipeline on multi-turn tool-use trajectories for locally-deployable CTF/security agents, bring-your-own components.

environment.name: Agent-World-Model
environment.takeaway: 1,000 executable, SQL-database-backed synthetic tool-use environments (avg 35 tools each) exposed through a unified MCP interface for large-scale agentic RL.

environment.name: TermiGen
environment.takeaway: 3,500+ verified executable Docker terminal environments with error-correction trajectory synthesis for training robust terminal/tool-use agents.

environment.name: VisGym
environment.takeaway: A gymnasium of 17 multimodal environments with controllable difficulty, input representation, horizon, and feedback for training and evaluating VLM agents.

environment.name: NeMo-Gym
environment.takeaway: Manages the full stateful agent system (data, harness, verifier, exec context) so evaluation, optimization, and RL share one reproducible pipeline at scale.

environment.name: VISTA-Gym
environment.takeaway: A tool-integrated visual-reasoning environment (detection, segmentation, OCR, crop) across 13 datasets, used to scale agentic RL for VLMs.

environment.name: LoCoBench-Agent
environment.takeaway: Turns 8K static code scenarios into interactive multi-turn envs to test long-context (10K-1M tokens) SWE-agent behavior across languages.

environment.name: Simia-Agent-Training
environment.takeaway: Uses reasoning models to simulate realistic environments, then trains tool-use agents via SFT and RL on those synthetic trajectories instead of real systems.

environment.name: PaperArena
environment.org: University of Science and Technology of China
environment.takeaway: Benchmark for research questions needing cross-paper synthesis via orchestrated external tools (PDF parsing, retrieval, computation).

environment.name: enterprise-deep-research
environment.takeaway: Multi-agent deep-research system combining adaptive query decomposition, specialized search agents, reflection, and human-in-the-loop steering.

environment.name: meta-agents-research-environments
environment.takeaway: Gaia2 evaluation in evolving environments where new info and shifting conditions force agents to adapt strategies, adding temporal complexity to static benchmarks.

environment.name: BrowseComp-Plus
environment.takeaway: Evaluates deep-research agents against a fixed ~100K human-verified corpus to control retrieval and isolate retriever-vs-LLM effects fairly.

environment.name: MCP-Bench
environment.takeaway: Connects LLMs to 28 live MCP servers (250 tools) to test cross-domain orchestration, schema understanding, and fuzzy-instruction tool planning.

environment.name: MCPVerse
environment.takeaway: Benchmark of 550+ executable real-world tools used simultaneously, an OS-like environment for testing agents in large tool spaces.

environment.name: CompassVerifier
environment.takeaway: Verifier model classifying outputs as correct/incorrect/quality-problematic robustly across prompt formats, for consistent RL reward signals.

environment.name: tau2-bench
environment.takeaway: Dual-control simulation where agent and simulated user both act, testing policy adherence and tool use in customer-service dialogues.

environment.name: Mind2Web-2
environment.takeaway: Evaluates long-horizon agentic search with an Agent-as-a-Judge rubric scoring citation-backed synthesis of real-time information.

environment.name: MCP-Universe
environment.takeaway: Benchmarks agents against real live MCP servers and unfamiliar tool spaces over multi-step tasks rather than simplified synthetic tool calls.

environment.name: gem
environment.takeaway: Gym-style composable env API for LLM agents with async vectorization, tool integration (Python/search/MCP), and adapters to 6 RL frameworks.

environment.name: MLE-Dojo
environment.takeaway: Gym-style sandbox over 200+ Kaggle ML-engineering tasks giving both final-outcome and intermediate-step rewards via a FeedbackManager for SFT/RL.

environment.name: R2E-Gym
environment.takeaway: SWE-GEN builds executable training envs from commits (not human PRs) plus hybrid exec/exec-free verifiers for scalable real-world SWE-agent RL.

environment.name: SWE-smith
environment.takeaway: Toolkit that turns any GitHub repo into a SWE-gym, generating unlimited synthetic training tasks for SWE agents.

environment.name: atropos
environment.takeaway: Microservice framework for async LLM RL: envs run as decoupled services that tokenize/score rollouts into a shared trajectory API queue independent of trainers.

environment.name: InternBootcamp
environment.takeaway: Evolutionarily auto-generated 1000+ verifiable reasoning tasks (case_generator/prompt_func/verify_func) showing task-count scaling boosts RL reasoning via cross-task transfer.

environment.name: loong
environment.takeaway: Self-bootstrapping RLVR loop (Generator/Verifier/Agent) over 8729 seed questions in 12 computable domains using executable rationales for deterministic reward.

environment.name: DataSciBench
environment.takeaway: Benchmark evaluating LLM agents on multi-step interdependent data-science workflows (plan, code, sequential execution) via completion-rate, cost, and error metrics.

environment.name: reasoning-gym
environment.takeaway: 100+ procedural dataset generators with built-in score_answer functions giving infinite difficulty-adjustable, algorithmically verifiable rewards for RL reasoning training.

environment.name: llmgym
environment.takeaway: Gymnasium-style unified reset/step interface bundling diverse LLM-app environments (QA, customer service, terminal) with built-in feedback for RL/SFT benchmarking.

environment.name: SWE-Gym
environment.takeaway: First training environment of 2,438 executable real-world Python tasks for SWE agents via SFT plus inference-time verifier scaling.

environment.name: debug-gym
environment.takeaway: Interactive debugging env wrapping pdb plus bash/view/grep/edit/eval tools so agents debug via real breakpoints/state inspection, not just code synthesis.

environment.name: gym-llm
environment.takeaway: LLMEnv adapter exposing gymnasium control/game envs to LLM agents via observation/action/goal schemas plus history-reflection, targeting planning over real-time control.

environment.name: AgentGym
environment.takeaway: 14 ReAct-format envs as HTTP services plus AgentEvol, a self-evolution method letting agents improve beyond seen data across tasks/environments.

environment.name: tau-bench
environment.takeaway: Benchmark for tool-agent-user interaction: LLM-simulated users hold dynamic multi-turn conversations testing API tool use under policy constraints (pass^k consistency).

environment.name: appworld
environment.takeaway: High-fidelity sandbox of 9 apps/457 APIs over ~100 simulated people where agents write interactive Python; success judged by database-state changes.

environment.name: android_world
environment.takeaway: Live-emulator benchmark of 116 tasks across 20 apps with parameter-randomized dynamic task instantiation and durable reward signals to prevent solution memorization.

environment.name: TheAgentCompany
environment.takeaway: Simulated software company (GitLab, PM, chat) benchmarking agents on consequential cross-role work tasks with checkpoint-based deterministic and LLM evaluators.

environment.name: LlamaGym
environment.takeaway: Single Agent base class abstracting context, episode batching, reward assignment, and PPO loops so LLMs can be online-RL fine-tuned in Gym with three methods.

environment.name: visualwebarena
environment.takeaway: Multimodal extension of WebArena with visually-grounded web tasks evaluated via screenshots and Set-of-Marks element marking for vision-language agents.

environment.name: LMRL-Gym
environment.takeaway: Eight multi-turn RL benchmarks (mazes, chess, Wordle, 20-questions) stressing credit assignment, trajectory stitching, and info-gathering with BC+ILQL/PPO baselines.

environment.name: OSWorld
environment.takeaway: Computer-use benchmark running real OSes (Ubuntu/Windows via VM/Docker/AWS) where screenshot-driven agents do open-ended GUI/file/web tasks with parallel evaluation.

environment.name: webarena
environment.takeaway: Self-hostable replicas of real sites (shop, GitLab, Reddit, maps) for reproducible web-agent eval scored by functional task outcomes, not surface metrics.

environment.name: AgentBench
environment.takeaway: First benchmark evaluating LLM-as-agent across 8 multi-turn interactive environments (OS, DB, knowledge graph, card game, ALFWorld, web shop/browse).

environment.name: WebShop
environment.takeaway: Simulated e-commerce env (1.18M products, 12k instructions) for instruction-following shopping supporting IL/RL/IL+RL with sim-to-real transfer to live sites.

environment.name: ScienceWorld
environment.takeaway: Text-based environment of 10 elementary-science task families (up to 1,386 variations) with tunable simplifications, benchmarking agent scientific reasoning.

environment.name: factorio-learning-environment
environment.takeaway: Open-ended Factorio benchmark where agents act via a Python-code REPL (observe output, generate program, get state feedback) for non-saturating evals.

environment.name: alfworld
environment.takeaway: Aligns parallel TextWorld and embodied ALFRED/THOR tasks so agents learn abstract high-level policies in text before transferring to 3D actuation.

environment.name: jericho
environment.takeaway: Python interface to real interactive-fiction games exposing object trees, vocab, and template action generators to tame combinatorial text action spaces.

environment.name: TextWorld
environment.takeaway: Procedurally generates text adventure games (tw-make) behind a Gym-like API to supply diverse, difficulty-controllable RL training scenarios.

under_review.title: CoEvolve: Training LLM Agents via Agent-Data Mutual Evolution
under_review.takeaway: CoEvolve: agent-data mutual evolution that mines forgetting/uncertainty signals from rollouts to synthesize new tasks and update the RL training distribution.

under_review.title: JoyAgents-R1: Joint Evolution Dynamics for Versatile Multi-LLM Agents with Reinforcement Learning
under_review.takeaway: HiMA-Ecom/HiMA-R1: variance-reduction GRPO that selects informative agent groups by reward variance for joint training of hierarchical e-commerce multi-agents.

under_review.title: Shop-R1: Rewarding LLMs to Simulate Human Behavior in Online Shopping via Reinforcement Learning
under_review.takeaway: Shop-R1: two-stage RL simulating shopper behavior via logit-guided rationale rewards plus difficulty-aware hierarchical action rewards to curb reward hacking.

under_review.title: Training Long-Context, Multi-Turn Software Engineering Agents with Reinforcement Learning
under_review.takeaway: Trains long-context multi-turn SWE agents via rejection fine-tuning then synchronous DAPO RL, lifting Qwen2.5-72B from 11% to 39% on SWE-bench Verified.

under_review.title: Acting Less is Reasoning More! Teaching Model to Act Efficiently
under_review.takeaway: OTC-PO: rewards correct answers with minimal tool calls (tool productivity) to fight cognitive offloading, cutting calls up to 68% while keeping accuracy.

under_review.title: Agentic Reasoning and Tool Integration for LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
under_review.takeaway: ARTIST: outcome-based RL teaching LLMs to autonomously decide when/which tools to invoke within multi-turn reasoning without step-level supervision.

under_review.title: ComputerRL: Scaling End-to-End Online Reinforcement Learning for Computer Use Agents
under_review.takeaway: ComputerRL: scales online RL over thousands of parallel desktops with an API-GUI action paradigm and Entropulse (RL/SFT alternation) to avoid entropy collapse.

under_review.title: Atom-Searcher: Enhancing Agentic Deep Research via Fine-Grained Atomic Thought Reward
under_review.takeaway: Atom-Searcher: decomposes reasoning into 'Atomic Thoughts' scored by a reasoning reward model for dense process-level rewards on a curriculum schedule.

under_review.title: MUA-RL: MULTI-TURN USER-INTERACTING AGENTREINFORCEMENT LEARNING FOR AGENTIC TOOL USE
under_review.takeaway: MUA-RL: multi-turn agentic tool-use RL with fresh per-rollout environments and a conversation schema controlling backprop for sustained agent-user dialogue.

under_review.title: Understanding Tool-Integrated Reasoning
under_review.takeaway: Understanding TIR: formal proof tools strictly expand LLM support, plus ASPO (Advantage Shaping Policy Optimization) that reshapes advantage to guide tool use.

under_review.title: Memory-R1: Enhancing Large Language Model Agents to Manage and Utilize Memories via Reinforcement Learning
under_review.takeaway: Memory-R1: outcome-driven RL (PPO/GRPO) trains a Memory Manager (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP) and Answer agent for learned external memory using only 152 QA pairs.

under_review.title: Encouraging Good Processes Without the Need for Good Answers: Reinforcement Learning for LLM Agent Planning
under_review.takeaway: RLTR: decouples agent planning from answering, rewarding tool-use completeness of invocation sequences so planning improves without verifiable final answers.

under_review.title: SFR-DeepResearch: Towards Effective Reinforcement Learning for Autonomously Reasoning Single Agents
under_review.takeaway: SFR-DeepResearch: simple RL recipe on entirely synthetic data that continually trains reasoning-optimized single agents while preserving reasoning ability.

under_review.title: WebExplorer: Explore and Evolve for Training Long-Horizon Web Agents
under_review.takeaway: WebExplorer: model-based exploration with long-to-short query evolution generates hard data for SFT+RL, training an 8B web agent over ~16 search turns.

under_review.title: EnvX: Agentize Everything with Agentic AI
under_review.takeaway: EnvX: agentizes GitHub repos via TODO-guided environment init and an Agent-to-Agent protocol for multi-repo collaboration; RL not detailed in abstract.

under_review.title: UI-TARS-2 Technical Report: Advancing GUI Agent with Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning
under_review.takeaway: UI-TARS-2: stabilized multi-turn RL for GUI agents with a data flywheel, hybrid file/terminal+GUI environments, and a unified sandbox for large-scale rollouts.

under_review.title: UI-Venus Technical Report: Building High-performance UI Agents with RFT
under_review.takeaway: UI-Venus: reinforcement fine-tuning of Qwen2.5-VL UI agents with grounding/navigation reward design plus self-evolving trajectory alignment and sparse-action enhancement.

under_review.title: Agent2 : An Agent-Generates-Agent Framework for Reinforcement Learning Automation
under_review.takeaway: Agent2: an LLM Generator Agent auto-designs/optimizes RL Target Agents by splitting development into MDP modeling and algorithmic optimization stages.

under_review.title: Adversarial Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Model Agent Safety
under_review.takeaway: ARLAS: trains LLM agent safety as a two-player zero-sum game with population-based learning defending against all prior attacker checkpoints to avoid cyclic dynamics.

under_review.title: Learning to Refine: An Agentic RL Approach for Iterative SPARQL Query Construction
under_review.takeaway: A 3B model learns iterative SPARQL query construction via outcome-only GRPO (no SFT), using real-time execution feedback to recover from errors and self-refine.

under_review.title: InfoFlow: Reinforcing Search Agent Via Reward Density Optimization
under_review.takeaway: InfoFlow combats sparse search rewards via reward-density optimization: subproblem intermediate rewards, corrective guidance on stalls, and a history-compressing refiner agent.
